steps.description: Click on the "Declarative Agent" option within the "New Project" dropdown menu to create an agent using instructions and actions.
Click on the "General Teams Agent" option within the dropdown menu titled "Teams Agent or App Using Teams AI Library" at coordinates (474, 210).
Click on the dropdown option "Tab" under the "Teams Capability" menu in the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit interface.
Click on the "Default folder" dropdown input field in the "Workspace Folder" dialog to select or specify the project directory.
Press the F1 key to open the Command Palette in Visual Studio Code, while viewing the "m365agents.yml" file in the Explorer panel.
Type text 'provision' into the Visual Studio Code Command Palette input box, filtering commands such as "Microsoft 365 Agents: Provision".
Click the "Microsoft 365 Agents: Provision" command in the Visual Studio Code command palette after searching for ">provision".
Click the "Cancel" button in the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit popup dialog within Visual Studio Code to dismiss the prompt for developer sandbox account setup.
Click the center area of the Visual Studio Code main workspace, where no UI panels or interactive elements are present, within the "MICROSOFT 365 AGENTS TOOLKIT" extension view.
Press the F1 key to open the command palette in Visual Studio Code, with the "MICROSOFT 365 AGENTS TOOLKIT" panel active on the left sidebar.
Type the text 'provision' into the Visual Studio Code command palette (top-center input field) within the Microsoft 365 Agents extension workspace.
Click the "Microsoft 365 Agent: Provision" command in the Visual Studio Code command palette, as indicated by the red crosshair at position (368, 52).
@assertion there is a popup shows sign in Azure account.
Click the "Sign in" button in the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit authentication popup within Visual Studio Code to initiate Azure account sign-in.
Click the "Allow" button on the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit permission dialog in Visual Studio Code to grant sign-in access.
Click the "Use another account" option in the Microsoft sign-in dialog on the login.microsoftonline.com page, indicated by the plus icon and text in the account picker interface.
@assertion   There is a visible prompt with the text "Select a resource group" .
